yaml.title: Complex Sampling Designs
yaml.subtitle: Multi-stage sampling, Design Effect, dan metode estimasi varians

markdown.markdown:
```{r} #| label: setup #| include: false source("_common.R") pop <- load_population() N <- nrow(pop) ``` Survei nasional jarang menggunakan SRS murni — mereka menerapkan **stratified multi-stage cluster sampling** yang mengkombinasikan beberapa teknik. Bab ini membahas bagaimana mendesain dan menganalisis survei kompleks tersebut [@lumley2010; @heeringa2017]. ### Two-Stage Sampling 1. **Stage 1 (PSU — Primary Sampling Unit):** Pilih cluster dari populasi. Contoh: kecamatan. 2. **Stage 2 (SSU — Secondary Sampling Unit):** Dari PSU terpilih, pilih subsample. Contoh: rumah tangga. ### Contoh: Desain Mirip SUSENAS BPS SUSENAS menggunakan desain stratified two-stage: - **Stratum:** provinsi × urban/rural - **PSU:** blok sensus (dipilih PPS berdasarkan jumlah rumah tangga) - **SSU:** 10 rumah tangga per blok sensus (dipilih systematic) ```{mermaid} %%| fig-width: 8 %%| label: fig-multistage flowchart TD A["Populasi
N = 10,000 RT
10 Provinsi, 50 Kab"] --> B["Stratifikasi
Province × Urban/Rural"] B --> C["Stage 1: Pilih District (PSU)
PPS berdasarkan jumlah RT"] C --> D["Stage 2: Pilih RT (SSU)
SRS 20 RT per district"] D --> E["Final Sample
n = selected PSU × 20"] style A fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb,stroke-width:2px style B fill:#ede9fe,stroke:#7c3aed,stroke-width:2px style C fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706,stroke-width:2px style D fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669,stroke-width:2px style E fill:#fce7f3,stroke:#db2777,stroke-width:2px ``` ### Implementasi Two-Stage Design ::: {.panel-tabset} #### R ```{r} #| label: two-stage-design #| fig-cap: "Two-stage sampling: 15 district (PSU) dipilih PPS, 20 RT (SSU) per district." #| fig-width: 9 #| fig-height: 5 set.seed(2024) # Stage 1: Select PSUs (districts) with PPS district_info <- pop |> summarise( N_i = n(), mean_income = mean(income), .by = c(province, district, urban_rural) ) |> mutate(prob_pps = N_i / sum(N_i)) n_psu <- 15 m_ssu <- 20 # SSU per PSU # PPS systematic selection cum_prob <- cumsum(district_info$prob_pps) interval <- 1 / n_psu start <- runif(1, 0, interval) select_pts <- start + (0:(n_psu - 1)) * interval psu_selected <- sapply(select_pts, function(p) { district_info$district[min(which(cum_prob >= p))] }) # Stage 2: SRS within each PSU two_stage_sample <- pop |> filter(district %in% psu_selected) |> slice_sample(n = m_ssu, by = district) # Setup survey design object two_stage_sample <- two_stage_sample |> left_join( district_info |> select(district, N_i, prob_pps), by = "district" ) |> mutate( psu_prob = n_psu * prob_pps, ssu_prob = m_ssu / N_i, final_weight = 1 / (psu_prob * ssu_prob) ) # Survey design des <- svydesign( id = ~district, strata = NULL, weights = ~final_weight, data = two_stage_sample, fpc = NULL ) # Estimates est_mean <- svymean(~income, des) est_total <- svytotal(~income, des) tibble( Parameter = c("Mean Income", "Total Income"), Estimate = c( format(round(coef(est_mean)), big.mark = ","), format(round(coef(est_total)), big.mark = ",") ), SE = c( format(round(SE(est_mean)), big.mark = ","), format(round(SE(est_total)), big.mark = ",") ), `CI Lower` = c( format(round(confint(est_mean)[1]), big.mark = ","), format(round(confint(est_total)[1]), big.mark = ",") ), `CI Upper` = c( format(round(confint(est_mean)[2]), big.mark = ","), format(round(confint(est_total)[2]), big.mark = ",") ) ) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Two-Stage Design Estimates (survey package)") ``` #### Python ```{python} #| label: two-stage-design-py #| fig-cap: "Two-stage sampling estimates (Python)." import numpy as np import pandas as pd np.random.seed(2024) pop_py = r.pop.copy() # Stage 1: Select districts dist_info = pop_py.groupby('district').agg( N_i=('income', 'size'), mean_income=('income', 'mean') ).reset_index() dist_info['prob'] = dist_info['N_i'] / dist_info['N_i'].sum() n_psu = 15 m_ssu = 20 cum_prob = dist_info['prob'].cumsum().values interval = 1 / n_psu start = np.random.uniform(0, interval) pts = start + np.arange(n_psu) * interval psu_sel = [dist_info.iloc[np.searchsorted(cum_prob, p)]['district'] for p in pts] # Stage 2: SRS within PSU samples = [] for d in psu_sel: d_data = pop_py[pop_py['district'] == d] s = d_data.sample(n=min(m_ssu, len(d_data)), random_state=2024) d_N = len(d_data) d_prob = dist_info[dist_info['district'] == d]['prob'].values[0] s = s.copy() s['weight'] = 1 / (n_psu * d_prob * m_ssu / d_N) samples.append(s) sample_2s = pd.concat(samples) # Weighted mean weighted_mean = np.average(sample_2s['income'], weights=sample_2s['weight']) true_mean = pop_py['income'].mean() results = pd.DataFrame({ 'Metric': ['Weighted Mean', 'True Mean', 'Difference', 'Sample Size'], 'Value': [f'{weighted_mean:,.0f}', f'{true_mean:,.0f}', f'{weighted_mean - true_mean:,.0f}', f'{len(sample_2s)}'] }) results ``` ::: ## Stratified Multi-Stage Cluster Sampling Desain survei nasional tipikal menggabungkan stratifikasi dengan multi-stage clustering: ::: {.panel-tabset} ### R ```{r} #| label: complex-design #| fig-cap: "Stratified two-stage cluster design menggunakan region × urban_rural sebagai strata." set.seed(2024) # Create strata pop <- pop |> mutate(stratum = paste(region, urban_rural, sep = "_")) strata_list <- unique(pop$stratum) n_psu_per_stratum <- 2 m_per_psu <- 15 # Stage 1: Select PSUs within each stratum complex_sample_list <- list() for (st in strata_list) { st_pop <- pop |> filter(stratum == st) st_districts <- unique(st_pop$district) if (length(st_districts) >= n_psu_per_stratum) { sel_psu <- sample(st_districts, n_psu_per_stratum) } else { sel_psu <- st_districts } # Stage 2: SRS within PSU st_sample <- st_pop |> filter(district %in% sel_psu) |> group_by(district) |> group_modify(~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(m_per_psu, nrow(.x)))) |> ungroup() |> mutate( stratum_id = st, psu_id = district, n_psu_in_stratum = length(sel_psu) ) complex_sample_list[[st]] <- st_sample } complex_sample <- bind_rows(complex_sample_list) # Compute weights (simplified) complex_sample <- complex_sample |> left_join( pop |> summarise(N_h = n(), .by = stratum), by = c("stratum_id" = "stratum") ) |> mutate( base_weight = N_h / n(), .by = stratum_id ) # Survey design des_complex <- svydesign( id = ~psu_id, strata = ~stratum_id, weights = ~base_weight, data = complex_sample, nest = TRUE ) # Estimates est_complex <- svymean(~income, des_complex) est_by_region <- svyby(~income, ~region, des_complex, svymean) est_by_region |> as_tibble() |> mutate( income = format(round(income), big.mark = ","), se = format(round(se), big.mark = ",") ) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Mean Income by Region (Complex Survey Design)") ``` ### Python ```{python} #| label: complex-design-py import numpy as np import pandas as pd pop_py = r.pop.copy() pop_py['stratum'] = pop_py['region'] + '_' + pop_py['urban_rural'] # Simplified complex sample results true_by_region = pop_py.groupby('region')['income'].mean().reset_index() true_by_region.columns = ['Region', 'True Mean Income'] true_by_region['True Mean Income'] = true_by_region['True Mean Income'].apply( lambda x: f'{x:,.0f}') true_by_region ``` ::: ## Design Effect (DEFF) & Effective Sample Size ### DEFF karena Clustering $$ \text{DEFF}_{cl} = 1 + (\bar{m} - 1)\rho $$ {#eq-deff-cl} di mana $\bar{m}$ = rata-rata unit per cluster, $\rho$ = ICC. ### DEFF karena Weighting $$ \text{DEFF}_w = 1 + \text{CV}(w)^2 $$ {#eq-deff-w} di mana $\text{CV}(w) = \text{SD}(w) / \text{mean}(w)$ adalah coefficient of variation dari weights. ### Combined DEFF $$ \text{DEFF} \approx \text{DEFF}_{cl} \times \text{DEFF}_w $$ {#eq-deff-combined} ### Effective Sample Size $$ n_{eff} = \frac{n}{\text{DEFF}} $$ {#eq-neff} ::: {.callout-warning} ## Interpretasi Praktis Jika $n = 1000$ dan $\text{DEFF} = 2.5$, maka $n_{eff} = 400$. Artinya, survei kompleks Anda memiliki presisi yang **setara dengan SRS berukuran 400** — bukan 1000. Ini memiliki implikasi besar terhadap sample size planning. ::: ### Menghitung DEFF ::: {.panel-tabset} #### R ```{r} #| label: fig-deff #| fig-cap: "Design Effect sebagai fungsi ICC dan cluster size: isolines menunjukkan DEFF = 1+(m-1)ρ." #| fig-width: 8 #| fig-height: 6 # DEFF contour plot rho_seq <- seq(0, 0.3, by = 0.005) m_seq <- seq(2, 50, by = 1) deff_grid <- expand_grid(rho = rho_seq, m = m_seq) |> mutate(DEFF = 1 + (m - 1) * rho) ggplot(deff_grid, aes(x = rho, y = m, z = DEFF)) + geom_contour_filled(breaks = c(1, 1.5, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 10, 15)) + geom_contour(breaks = c(1.5, 2, 3, 5, 10), color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) + scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma", name = "DEFF") + labs( title = "Design Effect: DEFF = 1 + (m-1)ρ", subtitle = "Bahkan ICC kecil (ρ=0.05) menghasilkan DEFF besar ketika cluster size besar", x = "ICC (ρ)", y = "Cluster Size (m)" ) ``` ```{r} #| label: tbl-deff-examples #| tbl-cap: "Contoh DEFF untuk kombinasi ICC dan cluster size yang umum." expand_grid( `ICC (ρ)` = c(0.01, 0.02, 0.05, 0.10, 0.20), `Cluster Size (m)` = c(5, 10, 20, 30, 50) ) |> mutate(DEFF = 1 + (`Cluster Size (m)` - 1) * `ICC (ρ)`) |> pivot_wider(names_from = `Cluster Size (m)`, values_from = DEFF, names_prefix = "m = ") |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "DEFF = 1 + (m-1)ρ") |> tab_spanner(label = "Cluster Size (m)", columns = starts_with("m =")) |> fmt_number(columns = starts_with("m ="), decimals = 2) ``` #### Python ```{python} #| label: fig-deff-py #| fig-cap: "Design Effect contour plot (Python)." #| fig-width: 8 #| fig-height: 6 import numpy as np import matplotlib.pyplot as plt rho = np.linspace(0, 0.3, 100) m = np.linspace(2, 50, 100) RHO, M = np.meshgrid(rho, m) DEFF = 1 + (M - 1) * RHO fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6)) cs = ax.contourf(RHO, M, DEFF, levels=[1, 1.5, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 10, 15], cmap='plasma') ax.contour(RHO, M, DEFF, levels=[1.5, 2, 3, 5, 10], colors='white', linewidths=0.5) plt.colorbar(cs, label='DEFF') ax.set_xlabel('ICC (ρ)') ax.set_ylabel('Cluster Size (m)') ax.set_title('Design Effect: DEFF = 1 + (m−1)ρ', fontweight='bold') plt.tight_layout() plt.show() ``` ::: ## Variance Estimation untuk Complex Designs Untuk desain kompleks, variance **tidak** bisa dihitung dengan formula SRS sederhana. Empat metode utama: ### Taylor Series Linearization (TSL) Metode default di `survey` package. Mengaproksimasi varians estimator non-linear menggunakan ekspansi Taylor orde pertama. $$ \hat{V}_{TSL}(\hat{\theta}) \approx \sum_{h=1}^H \frac{n_h}{n_h - 1} \sum_{i=1}^{n_h} (e_{hi} - \bar{e}_h)^2 $$ {#eq-tsl} di mana $e_{hi}$ = linearized residual dari PSU ke-$i$ di stratum $h$. ### Replication Methods | Metode | Prinsip | Jumlah Replikasi | Cocok untuk | |--------|---------|-----------------|-------------| | **BRR** | Half-sample replicates | $n_h = 2$ per stratum required | Desain paired PSU | | **Jackknife (JRR)** | Drop-one PSU | $\sum n_h$ replikasi | General, robust | | **Bootstrap** | Resample PSUs with replacement | B (biasanya 200-500) | Paling fleksibel | ### Implementasi Variance Estimation ::: {.panel-tabset} #### R ```{r} #| label: variance-estimation #| tbl-cap: "Perbandingan metode estimasi varians untuk desain survei kompleks." set.seed(2024) # Design with paired PSUs for BRR compatibility # Simplified: use 2 PSUs per stratum strata_v <- unique(pop$region) n_psu_v <- 2 # for BRR compatibility m_v <- 30 var_sample_list <- list() for (st in strata_v) { st_pop <- pop |> filter(region == st) dists <- unique(st_pop$district) sel <- sample(dists, min(n_psu_v, length(dists))) ss <- st_pop |> filter(district %in% sel) |> group_by(district) |> group_modify(~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(m_v, nrow(.x)))) |> ungroup() |> mutate(stratum_v = st, psu_v = district) var_sample_list[[st]] <- ss } var_sample <- bind_rows(var_sample_list) |> mutate(wt = N / n()) # TSL design des_tsl <- svydesign(id = ~psu_v, strata = ~stratum_v, weights = ~wt, data = var_sample, nest = TRUE) # JRR design des_jrr <- as.svrepdesign(des_tsl, type = "JKn") # Bootstrap design des_boot <- as.svrepdesign(des_tsl, type = "bootstrap", replicates = 200) # Estimates comparison tsl_est <- svymean(~income, des_tsl) jrr_est <- svymean(~income, des_jrr) boot_est <- svymean(~income, des_boot) tibble( Method = c("TSL (Taylor)", "Jackknife (JKn)", "Bootstrap (B=200)"), Estimate = c(coef(tsl_est), coef(jrr_est), coef(boot_est)), SE = c(SE(tsl_est), SE(jrr_est), SE(boot_est)) ) |> mutate( Estimate = format(round(Estimate), big.mark = ","), SE = format(round(SE), big.mark = ",") ) |> gt() |> tab_header( title = "Variance Estimation Methods Comparison", subtitle = "Estimasi mean income — semua metode menghasilkan SE yang serupa" ) ``` #### Python ```{python} #| label: variance-estimation-py import numpy as np import pandas as pd # Simplified comparison using manual jackknife sample_py = r.var_sample.copy() clusters = sample_py['psu_v'].unique() income_vals = sample_py['income'].values weights = sample_py['wt'].values # Weighted mean theta_hat = np.average(income_vals, weights=weights) # Jackknife: drop one cluster at a time jack_estimates = [] for c in clusters: mask = sample_py['psu_v'] != c jack_est = np.average(income_vals[mask], weights=weights[mask]) jack_estimates.append(jack_est) jack_estimates = np.array(jack_estimates) n_c = len(clusters) jack_var = ((n_c - 1) / n_c) * np.sum((jack_estimates - theta_hat)**2) jack_se = np.sqrt(jack_var) results = pd.DataFrame({ 'Metric': ['Weighted Mean', 'Jackknife SE', 'n clusters', 'n total'], 'Value': [f'{theta_hat:,.0f}', f'{jack_se:,.0f}', str(n_c), str(len(income_vals))] }) results ``` ::: ## Praktis: svydesign() di R ### Setup Lengkap ```{r} #| label: svydesign-practical #| code-fold: false # --- Step 1: Define the survey design --- # id = PSU variable (cluster identifier) # strata = stratification variable # weights = sampling weight (inverse selection probability) # nest = TRUE jika PSU IDs tidak unik across strata des <- svydesign( id = ~psu_v, strata = ~stratum_v, weights = ~wt, data = var_sample, nest = TRUE ) ``` ### Fungsi Analisis Utama ```{r} #| label: survey-functions # --- Means --- svymean(~income, des) # --- Totals --- svytotal(~income, des) # --- Proportions --- svymean(~urban_rural, des) # --- Quantiles --- svyquantile(~income, des, quantiles = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)) ``` ### Analisis by Subgroup ```{r} #| label: tbl-svyby #| tbl-cap: "Mean income by urban/rural menggunakan svyby()." # Correct approach: svyby (uses full design) svyby(~income, ~urban_rural, des, svymean) |> as_tibble() |> mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~format(round(.), big.mark = ","))) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Mean Income by Urban/Rural (Complex Design)") ``` ::: {.callout-warning} ## Common Pitfall: Subpopulation Analysis **SALAH:** Subset data dulu, baru buat survey design. ```r # ❌ SALAH — mengabaikan design structure urban_data <- var_sample |> filter(urban_rural == "Urban") des_wrong <- svydesign(id = ~psu_v, strata = ~stratum_v, weights = ~wt, data = urban_data) ``` **BENAR:** Gunakan `subset()` pada survey design object, atau `svyby()`. ```r # ✅ BENAR — preserves design information des_urban <- subset(des, urban_rural == "Urban") svymean(~income, des_urban) ``` Memfilter sebelum `svydesign()` menghapus informasi tentang PSU dan strata yang tidak terpilih, menghasilkan standard error yang **underestimated**. ::: ### Survey Regression ```{r} #| label: svyglm-demo #| tbl-cap: "Survey-weighted regression: income ~ urban_rural + education." model <- svyglm(income ~ urban_rural + education, design = des) broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) |> mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 2))) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Survey-Weighted OLS: Income ~ Urban/Rural + Education") ``` ### srvyr Alternative (Tidyverse-friendly) ```{r} #| label: srvyr-demo # srvyr: dplyr-like syntax for survey analysis des_srvyr <- var_sample |> as_survey_design( ids = psu_v, strata = stratum_v, weights = wt, nest = TRUE ) des_srvyr |> group_by(region) |> summarise( mean_income = survey_mean(income, vartype = c("se", "ci")), prop_urban = survey_mean(urban_rural == "Urban", vartype = "ci") ) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Region-level Estimates (srvyr)") |> fmt_number(columns = where(is.numeric), decimals = 0) ``` ::: {.takeaways} ### Key Takeaways 1. **Survei nasional menggunakan desain kompleks** (stratified multi-stage cluster) — BUKAN SRS. Analisis harus memperhitungkan design features. 2. **DEFF menghubungkan presisi aktual dengan presisi SRS.** DEFF = 2 berarti Anda membutuhkan sampel 2× lebih besar dari perhitungan SRS. 3. **Selalu gunakan `svydesign()`** (atau `as_survey_design()`) di R. Jangan pernah ignore weights, PSU, atau strata. 4. **Variance estimation methods** (TSL, JRR, Bootstrap) memberikan hasil serupa untuk statistik sederhana, tapi berbeda untuk statistik kompleks (quantile, medians). 5. **Subpopulation analysis:** gunakan `subset()` pada design object, bukan filter data sebelum design setup. ::: ::: {.exercises} ### Exercises 1. **Design setup.** Dengan dataset yang tersedia, buat survey design menggunakan `province` × `urban_rural` sebagai strata dan `district` sebagai PSU. Hitung mean income dan bandingkan SE dari `svymean()` vs. SE naif (tanpa design). 2. **DEFF calculation.** Hitung DEFF empiris untuk estimasi mean income pada desain di atas. Berapa effective sample size? Diskusikan implikasinya. 3. **Regression comparison.** Jalankan `glm(income ~ education + urban_rural)` tanpa survey design dan `svyglm()` dengan design. Bandingkan coefficients dan standard errors. Apa yang berubah dan mengapa? ::: ### Further Reading - @lumley2010: Panduan komprehensif analisis survei kompleks di R. - @heeringa2017: Pendekatan applied untuk analisis data survei. - @wolter2007: Teori mendalam tentang estimasi varians.

```{r} #| label: setup #| include: false source("_common.R") pop <- load_population() N <- nrow(pop) ``` Survei nasional jarang menggunakan SRS murni — mereka menerapkan **stratified multi-stage cluster sampling** yang mengkombinasikan beberapa teknik. Bab ini membahas bagaimana mendesain dan menganalisis survei kompleks tersebut [@lumley2010; @heeringa2017]. ## Multi-Stage Sampling ### Two-Stage Sampling 1. **Stage 1 (PSU — Primary Sampling Unit):** Pilih cluster dari populasi. Contoh: kecamatan. 2. **Stage 2 (SSU — Secondary Sampling Unit):** Dari PSU terpilih, pilih subsample. Contoh: rumah tangga. ### Contoh: Desain Mirip SUSENAS BPS SUSENAS menggunakan desain stratified two-stage: - **Stratum:** provinsi × urban/rural - **PSU:** blok sensus (dipilih PPS berdasarkan jumlah rumah tangga) - **SSU:** 10 rumah tangga per blok sensus (dipilih systematic) ```{mermaid} %%| fig-width: 8 %%| label: fig-multistage flowchart TD A["Populasi
N = 10,000 RT
10 Provinsi, 50 Kab"] --> B["Stratifikasi
Province × Urban/Rural"] B --> C["Stage 1: Pilih District (PSU)
PPS berdasarkan jumlah RT"] C --> D["Stage 2: Pilih RT (SSU)
SRS 20 RT per district"] D --> E["Final Sample
n = selected PSU × 20"] style A fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb,stroke-width:2px style B fill:#ede9fe,stroke:#7c3aed,stroke-width:2px style C fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706,stroke-width:2px style D fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669,stroke-width:2px style E fill:#fce7f3,stroke:#db2777,stroke-width:2px ``` ### Implementasi Two-Stage Design ::: {.panel-tabset} #### R ```{r} #| label: two-stage-design #| fig-cap: "Two-stage sampling: 15 district (PSU) dipilih PPS, 20 RT (SSU) per district." #| fig-width: 9 #| fig-height: 5 set.seed(2024) # Stage 1: Select PSUs (districts) with PPS district_info <- pop |> summarise( N_i = n(), mean_income = mean(income), .by = c(province, district, urban_rural) ) |> mutate(prob_pps = N_i / sum(N_i)) n_psu <- 15 m_ssu <- 20 # SSU per PSU # PPS systematic selection cum_prob <- cumsum(district_info$prob_pps) interval <- 1 / n_psu start <- runif(1, 0, interval) select_pts <- start + (0:(n_psu - 1)) * interval psu_selected <- sapply(select_pts, function(p) { district_info$district[min(which(cum_prob >= p))] }) # Stage 2: SRS within each PSU two_stage_sample <- pop |> filter(district %in% psu_selected) |> slice_sample(n = m_ssu, by = district) # Setup survey design object two_stage_sample <- two_stage_sample |> left_join( district_info |> select(district, N_i, prob_pps), by = "district" ) |> mutate( psu_prob = n_psu * prob_pps, ssu_prob = m_ssu / N_i, final_weight = 1 / (psu_prob * ssu_prob) ) # Survey design des <- svydesign( id = ~district, strata = NULL, weights = ~final_weight, data = two_stage_sample, fpc = NULL ) # Estimates est_mean <- svymean(~income, des) est_total <- svytotal(~income, des) tibble( Parameter = c("Mean Income", "Total Income"), Estimate = c( format(round(coef(est_mean)), big.mark = ","), format(round(coef(est_total)), big.mark = ",") ), SE = c( format(round(SE(est_mean)), big.mark = ","), format(round(SE(est_total)), big.mark = ",") ), `CI Lower` = c( format(round(confint(est_mean)[1]), big.mark = ","), format(round(confint(est_total)[1]), big.mark = ",") ), `CI Upper` = c( format(round(confint(est_mean)[2]), big.mark = ","), format(round(confint(est_total)[2]), big.mark = ",") ) ) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Two-Stage Design Estimates (survey package)") ``` #### Python ```{python} #| label: two-stage-design-py #| fig-cap: "Two-stage sampling estimates (Python)." import numpy as np import pandas as pd np.random.seed(2024) pop_py = r.pop.copy() # Stage 1: Select districts dist_info = pop_py.groupby('district').agg( N_i=('income', 'size'), mean_income=('income', 'mean') ).reset_index() dist_info['prob'] = dist_info['N_i'] / dist_info['N_i'].sum() n_psu = 15 m_ssu = 20 cum_prob = dist_info['prob'].cumsum().values interval = 1 / n_psu start = np.random.uniform(0, interval) pts = start + np.arange(n_psu) * interval psu_sel = [dist_info.iloc[np.searchsorted(cum_prob, p)]['district'] for p in pts] # Stage 2: SRS within PSU samples = [] for d in psu_sel: d_data = pop_py[pop_py['district'] == d] s = d_data.sample(n=min(m_ssu, len(d_data)), random_state=2024) d_N = len(d_data) d_prob = dist_info[dist_info['district'] == d]['prob'].values[0] s = s.copy() s['weight'] = 1 / (n_psu * d_prob * m_ssu / d_N) samples.append(s) sample_2s = pd.concat(samples) # Weighted mean weighted_mean = np.average(sample_2s['income'], weights=sample_2s['weight']) true_mean = pop_py['income'].mean() results = pd.DataFrame({ 'Metric': ['Weighted Mean', 'True Mean', 'Difference', 'Sample Size'], 'Value': [f'{weighted_mean:,.0f}', f'{true_mean:,.0f}', f'{weighted_mean - true_mean:,.0f}', f'{len(sample_2s)}'] }) results ``` ::: ## Stratified Multi-Stage Cluster Sampling Desain survei nasional tipikal menggabungkan stratifikasi dengan multi-stage clustering: ::: {.panel-tabset} ### R ```{r} #| label: complex-design #| fig-cap: "Stratified two-stage cluster design menggunakan region × urban_rural sebagai strata." set.seed(2024) # Create strata pop <- pop |> mutate(stratum = paste(region, urban_rural, sep = "_")) strata_list <- unique(pop$stratum) n_psu_per_stratum <- 2 m_per_psu <- 15 # Stage 1: Select PSUs within each stratum complex_sample_list <- list() for (st in strata_list) { st_pop <- pop |> filter(stratum == st) st_districts <- unique(st_pop$district) if (length(st_districts) >= n_psu_per_stratum) { sel_psu <- sample(st_districts, n_psu_per_stratum) } else { sel_psu <- st_districts } # Stage 2: SRS within PSU st_sample <- st_pop |> filter(district %in% sel_psu) |> group_by(district) |> group_modify(~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(m_per_psu, nrow(.x)))) |> ungroup() |> mutate( stratum_id = st, psu_id = district, n_psu_in_stratum = length(sel_psu) ) complex_sample_list[[st]] <- st_sample } complex_sample <- bind_rows(complex_sample_list) # Compute weights (simplified) complex_sample <- complex_sample |> left_join( pop |> summarise(N_h = n(), .by = stratum), by = c("stratum_id" = "stratum") ) |> mutate( base_weight = N_h / n(), .by = stratum_id ) # Survey design des_complex <- svydesign( id = ~psu_id, strata = ~stratum_id, weights = ~base_weight, data = complex_sample, nest = TRUE ) # Estimates est_complex <- svymean(~income, des_complex) est_by_region <- svyby(~income, ~region, des_complex, svymean) est_by_region |> as_tibble() |> mutate( income = format(round(income), big.mark = ","), se = format(round(se), big.mark = ",") ) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Mean Income by Region (Complex Survey Design)") ``` ### Python ```{python} #| label: complex-design-py import numpy as np import pandas as pd pop_py = r.pop.copy() pop_py['stratum'] = pop_py['region'] + '_' + pop_py['urban_rural'] # Simplified complex sample results true_by_region = pop_py.groupby('region')['income'].mean().reset_index() true_by_region.columns = ['Region', 'True Mean Income'] true_by_region['True Mean Income'] = true_by_region['True Mean Income'].apply( lambda x: f'{x:,.0f}') true_by_region ``` ::: ## Design Effect (DEFF) & Effective Sample Size ### DEFF karena Clustering $$ \text{DEFF}_{cl} = 1 + (\bar{m} - 1)\rho $$ {#eq-deff-cl} di mana $\bar{m}$ = rata-rata unit per cluster, $\rho$ = ICC. ### DEFF karena Weighting $$ \text{DEFF}_w = 1 + \text{CV}(w)^2 $$ {#eq-deff-w} di mana $\text{CV}(w) = \text{SD}(w) / \text{mean}(w)$ adalah coefficient of variation dari weights. ### Combined DEFF $$ \text{DEFF} \approx \text{DEFF}_{cl} \times \text{DEFF}_w $$ {#eq-deff-combined} ### Effective Sample Size $$ n_{eff} = \frac{n}{\text{DEFF}} $$ {#eq-neff} ::: {.callout-warning} ## Interpretasi Praktis Jika $n = 1000$ dan $\text{DEFF} = 2.5$, maka $n_{eff} = 400$. Artinya, survei kompleks Anda memiliki presisi yang **setara dengan SRS berukuran 400** — bukan 1000. Ini memiliki implikasi besar terhadap sample size planning. ::: ### Menghitung DEFF ::: {.panel-tabset} #### R ```{r} #| label: fig-deff #| fig-cap: "Design Effect sebagai fungsi ICC dan cluster size: isolines menunjukkan DEFF = 1+(m-1)ρ." #| fig-width: 8 #| fig-height: 6 # DEFF contour plot rho_seq <- seq(0, 0.3, by = 0.005) m_seq <- seq(2, 50, by = 1) deff_grid <- expand_grid(rho = rho_seq, m = m_seq) |> mutate(DEFF = 1 + (m - 1) * rho) ggplot(deff_grid, aes(x = rho, y = m, z = DEFF)) + geom_contour_filled(breaks = c(1, 1.5, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 10, 15)) + geom_contour(breaks = c(1.5, 2, 3, 5, 10), color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) + scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma", name = "DEFF") + labs( title = "Design Effect: DEFF = 1 + (m-1)ρ", subtitle = "Bahkan ICC kecil (ρ=0.05) menghasilkan DEFF besar ketika cluster size besar", x = "ICC (ρ)", y = "Cluster Size (m)" ) ``` ```{r} #| label: tbl-deff-examples #| tbl-cap: "Contoh DEFF untuk kombinasi ICC dan cluster size yang umum." expand_grid( `ICC (ρ)` = c(0.01, 0.02, 0.05, 0.10, 0.20), `Cluster Size (m)` = c(5, 10, 20, 30, 50) ) |> mutate(DEFF = 1 + (`Cluster Size (m)` - 1) * `ICC (ρ)`) |> pivot_wider(names_from = `Cluster Size (m)`, values_from = DEFF, names_prefix = "m = ") |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "DEFF = 1 + (m-1)ρ") |> tab_spanner(label = "Cluster Size (m)", columns = starts_with("m =")) |> fmt_number(columns = starts_with("m ="), decimals = 2) ``` #### Python ```{python} #| label: fig-deff-py #| fig-cap: "Design Effect contour plot (Python)." #| fig-width: 8 #| fig-height: 6 import numpy as np import matplotlib.pyplot as plt rho = np.linspace(0, 0.3, 100) m = np.linspace(2, 50, 100) RHO, M = np.meshgrid(rho, m) DEFF = 1 + (M - 1) * RHO fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 6)) cs = ax.contourf(RHO, M, DEFF, levels=[1, 1.5, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 10, 15], cmap='plasma') ax.contour(RHO, M, DEFF, levels=[1.5, 2, 3, 5, 10], colors='white', linewidths=0.5) plt.colorbar(cs, label='DEFF') ax.set_xlabel('ICC (ρ)') ax.set_ylabel('Cluster Size (m)') ax.set_title('Design Effect: DEFF = 1 + (m−1)ρ', fontweight='bold') plt.tight_layout() plt.show() ``` ::: ## Variance Estimation untuk Complex Designs Untuk desain kompleks, variance **tidak** bisa dihitung dengan formula SRS sederhana. Empat metode utama: ### Taylor Series Linearization (TSL) Metode default di `survey` package. Mengaproksimasi varians estimator non-linear menggunakan ekspansi Taylor orde pertama. $$ \hat{V}_{TSL}(\hat{\theta}) \approx \sum_{h=1}^H \frac{n_h}{n_h - 1} \sum_{i=1}^{n_h} (e_{hi} - \bar{e}_h)^2 $$ {#eq-tsl} di mana $e_{hi}$ = linearized residual dari PSU ke-$i$ di stratum $h$. ### Replication Methods | Metode | Prinsip | Jumlah Replikasi | Cocok untuk | |--------|---------|-----------------|-------------| | **BRR** | Half-sample replicates | $n_h = 2$ per stratum required | Desain paired PSU | | **Jackknife (JRR)** | Drop-one PSU | $\sum n_h$ replikasi | General, robust | | **Bootstrap** | Resample PSUs with replacement | B (biasanya 200-500) | Paling fleksibel | ### Implementasi Variance Estimation ::: {.panel-tabset} #### R ```{r} #| label: variance-estimation #| tbl-cap: "Perbandingan metode estimasi varians untuk desain survei kompleks." set.seed(2024) # Design with paired PSUs for BRR compatibility # Simplified: use 2 PSUs per stratum strata_v <- unique(pop$region) n_psu_v <- 2 # for BRR compatibility m_v <- 30 var_sample_list <- list() for (st in strata_v) { st_pop <- pop |> filter(region == st) dists <- unique(st_pop$district) sel <- sample(dists, min(n_psu_v, length(dists))) ss <- st_pop |> filter(district %in% sel) |> group_by(district) |> group_modify(~ slice_sample(.x, n = min(m_v, nrow(.x)))) |> ungroup() |> mutate(stratum_v = st, psu_v = district) var_sample_list[[st]] <- ss } var_sample <- bind_rows(var_sample_list) |> mutate(wt = N / n()) # TSL design des_tsl <- svydesign(id = ~psu_v, strata = ~stratum_v, weights = ~wt, data = var_sample, nest = TRUE) # JRR design des_jrr <- as.svrepdesign(des_tsl, type = "JKn") # Bootstrap design des_boot <- as.svrepdesign(des_tsl, type = "bootstrap", replicates = 200) # Estimates comparison tsl_est <- svymean(~income, des_tsl) jrr_est <- svymean(~income, des_jrr) boot_est <- svymean(~income, des_boot) tibble( Method = c("TSL (Taylor)", "Jackknife (JKn)", "Bootstrap (B=200)"), Estimate = c(coef(tsl_est), coef(jrr_est), coef(boot_est)), SE = c(SE(tsl_est), SE(jrr_est), SE(boot_est)) ) |> mutate( Estimate = format(round(Estimate), big.mark = ","), SE = format(round(SE), big.mark = ",") ) |> gt() |> tab_header( title = "Variance Estimation Methods Comparison", subtitle = "Estimasi mean income — semua metode menghasilkan SE yang serupa" ) ``` #### Python ```{python} #| label: variance-estimation-py import numpy as np import pandas as pd # Simplified comparison using manual jackknife sample_py = r.var_sample.copy() clusters = sample_py['psu_v'].unique() income_vals = sample_py['income'].values weights = sample_py['wt'].values # Weighted mean theta_hat = np.average(income_vals, weights=weights) # Jackknife: drop one cluster at a time jack_estimates = [] for c in clusters: mask = sample_py['psu_v'] != c jack_est = np.average(income_vals[mask], weights=weights[mask]) jack_estimates.append(jack_est) jack_estimates = np.array(jack_estimates) n_c = len(clusters) jack_var = ((n_c - 1) / n_c) * np.sum((jack_estimates - theta_hat)**2) jack_se = np.sqrt(jack_var) results = pd.DataFrame({ 'Metric': ['Weighted Mean', 'Jackknife SE', 'n clusters', 'n total'], 'Value': [f'{theta_hat:,.0f}', f'{jack_se:,.0f}', str(n_c), str(len(income_vals))] }) results ``` ::: ## Praktis: svydesign() di R ### Setup Lengkap ```{r} #| label: svydesign-practical #| code-fold: false # --- Step 1: Define the survey design --- # id = PSU variable (cluster identifier) # strata = stratification variable # weights = sampling weight (inverse selection probability) # nest = TRUE jika PSU IDs tidak unik across strata des <- svydesign( id = ~psu_v, strata = ~stratum_v, weights = ~wt, data = var_sample, nest = TRUE ) ``` ### Fungsi Analisis Utama ```{r} #| label: survey-functions # --- Means --- svymean(~income, des) # --- Totals --- svytotal(~income, des) # --- Proportions --- svymean(~urban_rural, des) # --- Quantiles --- svyquantile(~income, des, quantiles = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)) ``` ### Analisis by Subgroup ```{r} #| label: tbl-svyby #| tbl-cap: "Mean income by urban/rural menggunakan svyby()." # Correct approach: svyby (uses full design) svyby(~income, ~urban_rural, des, svymean) |> as_tibble() |> mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~format(round(.), big.mark = ","))) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Mean Income by Urban/Rural (Complex Design)") ``` ::: {.callout-warning} ## Common Pitfall: Subpopulation Analysis **SALAH:** Subset data dulu, baru buat survey design. ```r # ❌ SALAH — mengabaikan design structure urban_data <- var_sample |> filter(urban_rural == "Urban") des_wrong <- svydesign(id = ~psu_v, strata = ~stratum_v, weights = ~wt, data = urban_data) ``` **BENAR:** Gunakan `subset()` pada survey design object, atau `svyby()`. ```r # ✅ BENAR — preserves design information des_urban <- subset(des, urban_rural == "Urban") svymean(~income, des_urban) ``` Memfilter sebelum `svydesign()` menghapus informasi tentang PSU dan strata yang tidak terpilih, menghasilkan standard error yang **underestimated**. ::: ### Survey Regression ```{r} #| label: svyglm-demo #| tbl-cap: "Survey-weighted regression: income ~ urban_rural + education." model <- svyglm(income ~ urban_rural + education, design = des) broom::tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE) |> mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~round(., 2))) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Survey-Weighted OLS: Income ~ Urban/Rural + Education") ``` ### srvyr Alternative (Tidyverse-friendly) ```{r} #| label: srvyr-demo # srvyr: dplyr-like syntax for survey analysis des_srvyr <- var_sample |> as_survey_design( ids = psu_v, strata = stratum_v, weights = wt, nest = TRUE ) des_srvyr |> group_by(region) |> summarise( mean_income = survey_mean(income, vartype = c("se", "ci")), prop_urban = survey_mean(urban_rural == "Urban", vartype = "ci") ) |> gt() |> tab_header(title = "Region-level Estimates (srvyr)") |> fmt_number(columns = where(is.numeric), decimals = 0) ``` ::: {.takeaways} ### Key Takeaways 1. **Survei nasional menggunakan desain kompleks** (stratified multi-stage cluster) — BUKAN SRS. Analisis harus memperhitungkan design features. 2. **DEFF menghubungkan presisi aktual dengan presisi SRS.** DEFF = 2 berarti Anda membutuhkan sampel 2× lebih besar dari perhitungan SRS. 3. **Selalu gunakan `svydesign()`** (atau `as_survey_design()`) di R. Jangan pernah ignore weights, PSU, atau strata. 4. **Variance estimation methods** (TSL, JRR, Bootstrap) memberikan hasil serupa untuk statistik sederhana, tapi berbeda untuk statistik kompleks (quantile, medians). 5. **Subpopulation analysis:** gunakan `subset()` pada design object, bukan filter data sebelum design setup. ::: ::: {.exercises} ### Exercises 1. **Design setup.** Dengan dataset yang tersedia, buat survey design menggunakan `province` × `urban_rural` sebagai strata dan `district` sebagai PSU. Hitung mean income dan bandingkan SE dari `svymean()` vs. SE naif (tanpa design). 2. **DEFF calculation.** Hitung DEFF empiris untuk estimasi mean income pada desain di atas. Berapa effective sample size? Diskusikan implikasinya. 3. **Regression comparison.** Jalankan `glm(income ~ education + urban_rural)` tanpa survey design dan `svyglm()` dengan design. Bandingkan coefficients dan standard errors. Apa yang berubah dan mengapa? ::: ### Further Reading - @lumley2010: Panduan komprehensif analisis survei kompleks di R. - @heeringa2017: Pendekatan applied untuk analisis data survei. - @wolter2007: Teori mendalam tentang estimasi varians.

metadata.title: Complex Sampling Designs
metadata.subtitle: Multi-stage sampling, Design Effect, dan metode estimasi varians
